sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.36 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: The free Burnside group of exponent four on three generators,$B(3,4)$, has order$2^{69}$as shown by Bayes, Kautsky, and Wamsley (1974). Their proof is based on a theorem of Sanov (1940) which shows that$B(n,4)$is finite. Sanov's proof for$B(3,4)$uses more than$2^{32}$fourth powers, because the subgroup of$B(3,4)$generated by two of its generators and the square of the third has order$2^{32}$. It i...
